papers.title: Glycaemic control and pregnancy outcomes with real-time continuous glucose monitoring in gestational diabetes (GRACE): an open-label, multicentre, multinational, randomised controlled trial
papers.title_ja: 妊娠糖尿病に対するリアルタイム持続血糖モニタリングは、通常の自己血糖測定と比較して巨大児（LGA）の出生割合を有意に減少させる
papers.authors: Linder T, Dressler-Steinbach I, Wegener S, et al.
papers.abstract_en:
Background Data regarding the impact of real-time continuous glucose monitoring (rt-CGM) on reducing adverse pregnancy outcomes in women with gestational diabetes are contradictory. We aimed to assess differences in the proportion of large-for-gestational-age (LGA) newborns between women using rt-CGM versus self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG).
Methods For this open-label, parallel-group, multicentre, randomised controlled trial, women aged 18–55 years with singleton pregnancy and gestational diabetes (diagnosed according to the International Association of the Diabetes and Pregnancy Study Groups criteria), were randomly assigned (1:1) to rt-CGM or SMBG.
Findings Between Aug 24, 2020, and May 30, 2024, 610 women were screened for eligibility, of whom 375 were randomly assigned to rt-CGM (n=190) or SMBG (n=185). LGA neonates were born to six (4%) of 170 rt-CGM and 18 (10%) of 175 SMBG participants (OR 0·32, 95% CI 0·10–0·87, p=0·014).
Interpretation rt-CGM use in women with gestational diabetes reduced LGA births, without differences in serious adverse events. The higher-than-expected overall prevalence of SGA infants, possibly related to the tight glycaemic control in our cohort, requires further research.

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: 妊娠糖尿病を有する女性において、リアルタイム持続血糖モニタリング（rt-CGM）が有害な妊娠アウトカムを低減させる効果については相反するデータが存在する。本研究では、rt-CGMを使用する女性と自己血糖測定（SMBG）を行う女性の間で、巨大児（LGA）の出生割合に差があるかを評価することを目的とした。
abstract_sections.content_en: Data regarding the impact of real-time continuous glucose monitoring (rt-CGM) on reducing adverse pregnancy outcomes in women with gestational diabetes are contradictory. We aimed to assess differences in the proportion of large-for-gestational-age (LGA) newborns between women using rt-CGM versus self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG).

abstract_sections.label: 方法
abstract_sections.content: このオープンラベル並行群間多施設共同ランダム化比較試験において、18〜55歳で単胎妊娠かつ妊娠糖尿病（IADPSG基準に基づき診断）と診断された女性を、rt-CGM群またはSMBG群に1対1でランダムに割り付けた。
abstract_sections.content_en: For this open-label, parallel-group, multicentre, randomised controlled trial, women aged 18–55 years with singleton pregnancy and gestational diabetes (diagnosed according to the International Association of the Diabetes and Pregnancy Study Groups criteria), were randomly assigned (1:1) to rt-CGM or SMBG.

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: 2020年8月24日から2024年5月30日までに610名の適格性がスクリーニングされ、そのうち375名がrt-CGM群（n=190）またはSMBG群（n=185）に割り付けられた。LGA新生児の出生は、rt-CGM群170名中6名（4%）、SMBG群175名中18名（10%）であった（オッズ比 0.32、95%信頼区間 0.10-0.87、p=0.014）。
abstract_sections.content_en: Between Aug 24, 2020, and May 30, 2024, 610 women were screened for eligibility, of whom 375 were randomly assigned to rt-CGM (n=190) or SMBG (n=185). LGA neonates were born to six (4%) of 170 rt-CGM and 18 (10%) of 175 SMBG participants (OR 0·32, 95% CI 0·10–0·87, p=0·014).

abstract_sections.label: 結論
abstract_sections.content: 妊娠糖尿病女性におけるrt-CGMの使用は、重篤な有害事象に差を認めず、LGA出生を減少させた。本コホートにおける厳格な血糖管理に関連している可能性のある、予想以上に高いSGA児の有病率は、さらなる研究が必要である。
abstract_sections.content_en: rt-CGM use in women with gestational diabetes reduced LGA births, without differences in serious adverse events. The higher-than-expected overall prevalence of SGA infants, possibly related to the tight glycaemic control in our cohort, requires further research.

pico.P: 妊娠糖尿病と診断された18〜55歳の単胎妊娠の女性
pico.I: リアルタイム持続血糖モニタリング（rt-CGM）
pico.O: 巨大児（LGA）の出生割合（rt-CGM群 4% vs SMBG群 10%, OR 0.32, 95% CI 0.10–0.87, p=0.014）
